paragraphs.text: Using SRD-46 data at matched conditions (aqueous, 25 °C, ionic strength 0.1 M), **glycine and alanine have essentially the same lower pKa, but alanine has a modestly higher upper pKa**.

claims.text: glycine and alanine have essentially the same lower pKa
alanine has a modestly higher upper pKa

paragraphs.text: So, **not dramatically different overall**. The **carboxyl pKa is identical** in the matched SRD-46 entries, while the **amino-group pKa differs by 0.14 units**, which is small but a bit larger than typical experimental uncertainty often cited for good pKa measurements (~0.05–0.10). Chemically, that makes sense: the methyl group in alanine is slightly electron-donating relative to glycine, which can slightly increase the basicity of the amino group.

claims.text: The **carboxyl pKa is identical** in the matched SRD-46 entries,
a bit larger than typical experimental uncertainty
the methyl group in alanine is slightly electron-donating relative to glycine
which can slightly increase the basicity of the amino group.
